_comment: Adaptation measures catalog. All cost and effectiveness values are evidence-based with traceable sources.
_methodology: Cost estimates sourced from FEMA BCA Reference Guide (2019) and UK Environment Agency FCERM appraisal guidance. Damage reduction effectiveness from peer-reviewed literature. Design life from manufacturer specifications and FEMA guidance.

hazus_wind.citation: FEMA HAZUS MH Hurricane Technical Manual

iea_buildings.citation: IEA (2022) Buildings — Tracking Clean Energy Progress

who_heat.citation: WHO (2021) Climate change and heat: factsheet

ibhs_wind.citation: Insurance Institute for Business & Home Safety (IBHS) FORTIFIED standard

measures.label: Temporary Flood Barriers
measures.description: Deployable flood barriers (aqua dams, door guards) to exclude floodwater up to ~0.6m depth.
measures.effectiveness_source: FEMA (2019) BCA Guide Table 5-3: temporary barriers reduce flood damage 35–55%
measures.cost_source: FEMA BCA Reference Guide; typical installed cost 0.3–0.8% of structure value

measures.label: Permanent Flood Barrier / Bund
measures.description: Permanent earthen or reinforced concrete bund / perimeter wall, typically 0.6–1.5m height.
measures.effectiveness_source: EA FCERM (2020): permanent barriers provide 60–75% damage reduction for contained events
measures.cost_source: Environment Agency FCERM unit costs; EA/Defra FDGiA economic appraisal

measures.label: Elevated Foundation (Raise Floor Level)
measures.description: Raise ground floor level 0.5–1.5m above base flood elevation. Most effective measure for residential buildings.
measures.effectiveness_source: FEMA BCA Guide Table 5-1: elevation above BFE reduces structural damage 65–80%
measures.cost_source: FEMA HAZUS elevation cost functions; avg ~3–4% of structure value for 1m raise

measures.label: Structural Waterproofing (Tanking)
measures.description: Waterproof tanking, flood-proof door/window sets, and water-resistant coatings to reduce ingress.
measures.effectiveness_source: EA FCERM (2020) Property flood resilience round 2: dry waterproofing reduces damage 25–45%
measures.cost_source: RICS Building Cost Information Service; EA property-level flood resilience costs

measures.label: Sump Pump System
measures.description: Automatic sump pump with battery backup and overflow alarm for groundwater and minor ingress.
measures.effectiveness_source: FEMA BCA Guide: sump/drainage systems reduce basement flood damage ~15–25%
measures.cost_source: FEMA BCA Reference Guide; HomeAdvisor national cost database

measures.label: Permeable Paving & SuDS
measures.description: Permeable surfaces and Sustainable Drainage Systems (SuDS) to reduce surface-water runoff peak.
measures.effectiveness_source: CIRIA SuDS Manual C753 (2015): pervious surfaces reduce peak runoff 10–25%
measures.cost_source: CIRIA C753; Defra SuDS cost database

measures.label: Roof Reinforcement (Hurricane Straps)
measures.description: Hurricane straps, additional nail patterns, and reinforced roof-to-wall connections to resist uplift.
measures.effectiveness_source: IBHS FORTIFIED Home: roof connection upgrades reduce damage ~30% at design wind speed
measures.cost_source: FEMA HAZUS Hurricane Technical Manual; IBHS cost-effectiveness analysis

measures.label: Storm Shutters / Impact-Resistant Glazing
measures.description: Impact-resistant shutters or glazing protects openings from wind-borne debris; prevents internal pressure failure.
measures.effectiveness_source: IBHS FORTIFIED Home: opening protection reduces total damage 20–30%
measures.cost_source: IBHS; FEMA HAZUS Hurricane Technical Manual cost tables

measures.label: Foundation Anchor Bolts
measures.description: Structural anchor bolts securing sill plate/frame to foundation reduce lateral and uplift displacement.
measures.effectiveness_source: FEMA HAZUS Hurricane Technical Manual: foundation anchoring reduces total loss 15–25%

measures.label: Wind-Rated Curtain Walling / Glazing
measures.description: High-performance curtain walling or glazing system rated to design wind pressures (BS 6375 / ASTM E1996).
measures.effectiveness_source: FEMA HAZUS Hurricane: commercial glazing upgrades reduce envelope damage 30–40%
measures.cost_source: BCIS commercial glazing cost data; FEMA HAZUS cost tables

measures.label: Ember-Resistant Vents (1/16" mesh)
measures.description: Replace standard vents with 1/16" or finer mesh ember-resistant vents per NFPA 1144 / CAL FIRE guidelines.
measures.effectiveness_source: Syphard et al. (2012) Ecosphere: ember entry via vents accounts for ~20% of ignition risk
measures.cost_source: CAL FIRE Home Hardening Guide; IBHS Wildfire Prepared Home

measures.label: Non-Combustible Exterior Cladding
measures.description: Replace combustible cladding with Class A fire-rated materials: fibre cement, brick, stucco, or steel cladding.
measures.effectiveness_source: Syphard et al. (2012): non-combustible cladding reduces structure loss probability ~45%; IBHS Wildfire Prepared Home
measures.cost_source: IBHS Wildfire Prepared Home; CAL FIRE Home Hardening Program cost data

measures.label: Defensible Space / Vegetation Clearance Zone
measures.description: Maintain 30m (Zone 1: 0–10m) and 100m (Zone 2: 10–30m) non-combustible buffer per NFPA 1144 / CAL FIRE.
measures.effectiveness_source: Syphard et al. (2012); Cohen (2000) USDA Forest Service: defensible space reduces ignition probability 30–40%
measures.cost_source: CAL FIRE / National Fire Protection Association NFPA 1144 maintenance guidance

measures.label: Exterior Sprinkler / Suppression System
measures.description: Rooftop and perimeter suppression sprinklers activated automatically or manually during wildfire approach.
measures.effectiveness_source: NFPA 13/13D sprinkler effectiveness; exterior suppression adds ~30% damage reduction

measures.label: Building Insulation Upgrade
measures.description: Improved wall (cavity/solid), roof, and floor insulation reduces cooling loads and thermal discomfort under peak heat.
measures.effectiveness_source: IEA (2022) Buildings Tracking: wall/roof insulation reduces cooling energy demand 20–35%
measures.cost_source: UK BEIS ECO4 scheme cost data; IEA Buildings sector cost curves

measures.label: Cool Roof Coating (High Albedo)
measures.description: High solar-reflectance coating or white TPO membrane. Reduces roof surface temperature by 20–50 °C, peak cooling load 10–20%.
measures.effectiveness_source: Akbari et al. (2009) Energy & Buildings; US DOE Cool Roof Rating Council: reduces cooling demand 10–20%
measures.cost_source: US DOE Cool Roof materials cost database; BCIS roofing costs

measures.label: High-Efficiency HVAC / Heat Pump Upgrade
measures.description: Replace ageing HVAC with high-COP heat pump or VRF system sized for 2050 peak temperatures.
measures.effectiveness_source: IEA (2022) Heat Pumps Tracking: modern heat pumps 2–4× more efficient; reduces heat-stress productivity loss 35–45%
measures.cost_source: IEA Buildings; ASHRAE HVAC system cost estimating guide

measures.label: Green / Living Roof
measures.description: Vegetated extensive or intensive roof reduces surface temperature 2–8 °C via evapotranspiration.
measures.effectiveness_source: Susca et al. (2011) Atmospheric Environment: green roofs reduce summertime energy use 15–25%
measures.cost_source: Green Roof Organisation (UK); Whole Life Costs for Green Roofs (GRO, 2014)

measures.label: External Solar Shading (Brise Soleil / Louvres)
measures.description: Fixed or adjustable external brise soleil, horizontal fins, or louvres on south/west facades to reduce solar gain.
measures.effectiveness_source: CIBSE AM10 (2005) Natural ventilation: external shading reduces solar gain 60–90%, cutting cooling load 10–20%
measures.cost_source: BCIS commercial facade cost data; CIBSE shading system guidance
